articles.description:
Shake Shack's Korean-Style Menu is back for a limited time and includes two new additions: K-Shack Fried Chicken Bites and the K-Shack Spicy Caramel Shake.

The menu is available now when ordering online through the Shake Shack app or website ahead of a nationwide launch on January 9, 2026.
K-Shack Fried Chicken Bites features crispy-fried white-meat chicken bites coated in a spicy-sweet sesame gochujang glaze and sprinkled with fresh scallions. They're served with your choice of dipping sauce. 
The K-Shack Spicy Caramel Shake is a milkshake made with spicy gochujang caramel frozen custard topped with whipped cream and a drizzle of gochujang caramel.
Returning Korean-Style Menu items include:
- K-Shack Fried Chicken Sandwich - A crispy-fried chicken breast filet glazed in spicy-sweet sesame gochujang and served with fresh scallions and kimchi slaw made with Choi’s Kimchi, on a toasted potato bun. 
- K-Shack BBQ Burger - A quarter-pound Angus beef patty (or two or three), American cheese, crispy-fried sweet onions, fresh scallions, and Korean BBQ sauce, served on a toasted potato bun.
- K-Shack Spicy BBQ Fries - Crinkle-cut fries dusted with kimchi seasoning and served with a side of Korean BBQ sauce. Cheese Sauce can also be added for an additional charge.
Photo via Shake Shack.
Read more at Brand Eating!
articles.title: Korean-Style Menu Returns to Shake Shack with Two New Additions

articles.description:
KFC's Matty's Cheesy Nuggy Gravy Bowl features Secret Recipe Fries with gravy, topped with five Kentucky Fried Nuggets and five fried white cheddar Cheese Curds.

I paid $5 for the limited-time bowl.
The Cheesy Nuggy Gravy Bowl is KFC's and Chef Matty Matheson's take on poutine.



At first glance, I thought they might have forgotten the gravy in my bowl, but a closer examination revealed that the fries had absorbed much of the gravy. While the fries weren't crispy at all, they were thick enough to hold up well against the gravy. Additionally, the savory flavors of the two meshed very well, resulting in a surprising depth.



On the other hand, the Kentucky Fried Nuggets and the Cheese Curds retained a good deal of crispiness. The chicken nuggets were moist and meaty, but were a bit on the firm side. Flavor-wise, they're meant for dipping and don't offer much in the way of seasoning beyond a rather heavy dose of salt. The Cheese Curds were much better, with a similar flavor to mozzarella but with slightly more flavor. They were somewhat melted inside and, as they cooled, delivered a bit of the squeak that cheese curds are known for. Between the two, I preferred eating the nuggets alone, while the Cheese Curds went well with the fries and gravy.



Overall, the KFC Matty's Cheesy Nuggy Gravy Bowl delivered a delightful take on poutine in a country where poutine is a little bit hard to come by (at least here in Los Angeles) and certainly isn't on offer at any national fast-food chain.
Nutritional Info - KFC Matty's Cheesy Nuggy Gravy Bowl
Calories - 740
Fat - 39g (Saturated Fat - 11g)
Sodium - 2470mg
Carbs - 63g (Sugar - less than 1g)
Protein - 34g
Read more at Brand Eating!
articles.title: Review: KFC - Matty's Cheesy Nuggy Gravy Bowl

articles.description:
Popeyes starts off the new year by bringing back Wraps as part of the new $5 Faves Menu.

Available now for a limited time through February 15, 2026, at participating locations nationwide, the $5 Faves Menu is a value menu offering several options for $5 each ($6 each here in Los Angeles). They include:


Three pieces of Signature Chicken (breast pieces cost extra)
Three Tenders
Two regular sides (Homestyle Mac & Cheese costs extra)
Two Wraps


Popeyes Wraps feature a chicken tender wrapped in a soft flour tortilla with shredded lettuce, shredded cheese, and pickles. They come in four varieties: Classic (with a fried chicken tender and mayo), Spicy (with a Classic Tender and spicy mayo), Blackened Classic (with a Blackened Tender and mayo), and Blackened Spicy (with a Blackened Tender and spicy mayo).
Photo via Popeyes.
Read more at Brand Eating!
articles.title: Popeyes Brings Back Wraps as Part of New $5 Faves Value Menu

articles.description:
Baskin-Robbins introduces new Grahammy ice cream as its latest, limited-time flavor and Flavor of the Month for January 2026.

The new variety features graham cracker-flavored ice cream with chocolate-covered honeycomb pieces and honey graham cracker swirls, which amounts to plenty of honey and graham cracker flavors to go around.
The name is a play on the Grammy music awards and text that accompanies the new ice cream flavor reads, "Now this is a winner," and "this flavor is truly music to everyone's ears."
Photo via Baskin-Robbins.
Read more at Brand Eating!
articles.title: New Grahammy Ice Cream Arrives at Baskin-Robbins

articles.description:
Checkers & Rally's launches the new $5 MVP Meal Deal as their latest value meal offering.

Available for a limited time nationwide, the $5 MVP Meal Deal includes a choice of sandwich, value fries, a small soft drink, and a deep-fried Cinnamon Apple Pie. You can also opt for medium or large fries and a drink for an extra charge (the medium and large MVP Meal Deals cost $6.50 and $7, respectively, at a Rally's here in Los Angeles).
The sandwich choices for the value meal include:
- Mushroom Swissburger - A beef patty, melted Swiss cheese, and savory mushroom sauce on a toasted, bakery-style bun.
- Spicy Chicken Sandwich - A crispy-fried chicken patty, iceberg lettuce, and mayo, served on a toasted bakery-style bun.
- Cheese Double - Two beef patties, American cheese, ketchup, mustard, pickles, and onions, served on a toasted bakery-style bun.
Photo via Checkers & Rally's.
Read more at Brand Eating!
articles.title: Checkers & Rally's Puts Together New $5 MVP Meal Deal

articles.description:
KFC starts 2026 with the return of $5 Bowls for a limited time. It also debuts new Cheese Curds.

This round of $5 Bowls includes the following options:
- New Matty's Cheesy Nuggy Gravy Bowl - Created in partnership with Canadian chef/actor Matty Matheson, the Cheesy Nuggy Gravy Bowl takes inspiration from poutine and features Secret Recipe fries topped with chicken nuggets, fried cheese curds, and brown gravy.
- Mac & Cheese Bowl - Homestyle mac & cheese topped with chicken nuggets and a three-cheese blend (cheddar, mozzarella, and pepper jack).
- Spicy Mac & Cheese Bowl - Homestyle mac & cheese topped with chicken nuggets, a three-cheese blend, and Nashville Hot Sauce.
- Mashed Potato Bowl - Mashed potatoes topped with gravy, corn, chicken nuggets, and a three-cheese blend.
- Spicy Mashed Potato Bowl - Mashed potatoes topped with gravy, corn, chicken nuggets, a three-cheese blend, and Nashville Hot Sauce.
KFC's new Cheese Curds feature battered and fried Wisconsin-style white cheddar cheese curds. A 10-piece order costs $4.19 near me in Los Angeles.
Photo via KFC.
Read more at Brand Eating!
articles.title: KFC Brings Back $5 Bowls and Debuts New Cheese Curds

articles.description:
Dairy Queen launches two new Blizzards this month: the Oreo Snowdrift Blizzard and the Mocha Brownie Mudslide Blizzard. They're joined by the returning Choco-Dipped Strawberry Blizzard.

The Oreo Snowdrift Blizzard is a winter-themed treat that features vanilla soft serve mixed with Oreo cookie pieces and a sky-blue cake batter sauce, topped and stirred with a marshmallow sauce.
The Mocha Brownie Mudslide Blizzard is a coffee-inspired dessert that features vanilla soft serve blended with brownie pieces, chocolaty chunks, and coffee.
The Choco-Dipped Strawberry Blizzard evokes the warmer days of summer and features vanilla soft serve mixed with strawberry topping and chocolaty chunks.
All three Blizzards are available now for a limited time at participating locations nationwide.
Photo via Dairy Queen.
Read more at Brand Eating!
articles.title: Dairy Queen Welcomes New Oreo Snowdrift Blizzard and Mocha Brownie Mudslide Blizzard

articles.description:
As I mentioned earlier on the BrandEating Patreon, I received a Ninja Swirl Creami ice cream maker to experiment with courtesy of SharkNinja. I'm going to use it to see how and if various fast-food desserts could be made into ice cream or soft serve. I decided to start off with something easy: Turning Wendy's Chocolate Frosty into soft serve. It's almost soft serve already, so I figured, why not? I'm calling the segment, "Will it Creami?" ("Will it Swirl Creami" just doesn't sound right)

The Process
 I purchased a large Wendy's Frosty for $3.59 and let it melt slightly to reduce its volume. I then poured it into the Creami's supplied pint container and placed it into the freezer for a little over a day. After it was good and frozen, I ran it through the processing section of the Cremi (it took four minutes), which finely shaves and blends the frozen Frosty into a soft serve consistency (it's like the reverse of your typical ice cream maker, where you churn and freeze the ice cream at the same time).
Once it was churned, I attached the container to the soft serve dispensing section of the Creami. There, it was forced/pressed the Frosty soft serve through a nozzle to achieve that signature swirled look. If you don't want/need to have that swirled soft serve appearance, you can skip this step and just spoon the soft serve out of the container.



How it turned out
The Wendy's Frosty Soft Serve was pretty good. Obviously, it had the same mild chocolate and hint of vanilla flavor of a regular Frosty, but it also had the firmer, smoother, less melty texture of soft serve (my dispensing skills need improvement, however). It was a little too firm, though, and I wonder if that was due to the relatively low fat content of a Frosty (fat/cream makes ice cream softer) or if I should have given the soft serve another blend (there's a "re-spin" function on the machine). Still, the firmness wasn't outside the range of other soft serves that I've eaten.
Read more at Brand Eating!
articles.title: Will It Creami? Wendy's Chocolate Frosty

articles.description:
Taco Bell is testing "Everyday Nacho Fries" for a limited time at participating locations in the Charlotte, NC area.

Everyday Nacho Fries include Supreme Nacho Fries and Loaded Taco Nacho Fries. The test locations appear to be serving one or the other, but not both. Each is priced at $4.99.
Supreme Nacho Fries feature seasoned fries topped with seasoned beef, Spicy Ranch sauce, warm nacho cheese sauce, reduced-fat sour cream, diced tomatoes, and a 3-cheese blend (mozzarella, pepper jack, and cheddar).
Loaded Taco Nacho Fries feature seasoned fries topped with seasoned beef, warm nacho cheese sauce, shredded lettuce, diced tomatoes, shredded cheddar cheese, Spicy Ranch sauce, and crispy Fiesta Strips.
Loaded Taco Nacho Fries were previously released nationally as a limited-time item, while Supreme Nacho Fries are very similar to the limited-time Nacho Fries Supreme that accompanied the initial launch of Taco Bell's Nacho Fries. Given the "everyday" moniker, it looks like Taco Bell is exploring adding a topped Nacho Fries dish to its menu permanently.
Photo via Taco Bell.
Read more at Brand Eating!
